_note: Round-8 follow-up #3: `{:catch { name, message }}` destructure leaves should each type as `any` (matching upstream's `__sveltets_2_any({})` for CatchBlock owners). Two pre-fix bugs combined: (a) the parser's then/catch header scanner stopped at the first inner `}` of the destructure pattern (`{ name, message ` truncated), so the destructure was never even parsed; (b) the analyze-side AwaitCatch handler only resolved when `bindings.len() == 1`, so multi-leaf destructures dropped to None even after parsing. Fix: parser switches to depth-tracking via `find_mustache_end` so `{:catch { name, message }}` captures the full `{ name, message }` source slice; analyze drops the bindings.len() guard and resolves every leaf to `any`. Producer here exposes `{:catch { name, message }}` and `<slot {name} {message} />`; Consumer's `<Producer let:name let:message>` types both as `any` and the `takesAny(...)` calls pass. Pre-fix the slot literal would have been `{}` (both shorthand attrs dropped because their bindings never reached the shadow stack); consumer-side `let:name`/`let:message` would have typed as `any` via the unresolved-shadow path anyway, but the producer-side slot-attrs would have been silently dropped from the emitted slot literal.
